critical_issues: The customer's name 'Supriya Mulakkal' was incorrectly recorded as 'Supriya Molaka' in the MER.
The MER incorrectly states 'No' for medical tests (Q16a) despite the customer disclosing childbirth-related tests.
The doctor failed to explicitly ask questions regarding 'Annual Health Checkup' (Q15a, Q15b, Q15c) as per the MER template.
The doctor did not explicitly ask for the customer's agreement to the declaration at the end of the verification process.
recommendations: The doctor should ensure the customer's full name is accurately captured and verified against official documents.
The doctor must accurately record all disclosed medical information, including details of medical tests, even if results are normal, in the MER.
The doctor should ensure all questions from the MER template, including those about annual health checkups, are explicitly asked to the customer.
The doctor should explicitly read out the declaration and seek the customer's agreement as per the MER documentation.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer was cooperative and answered all questions clearly.
Doctor maintained a polite and clear tone throughout the call.
